message:
perf(fr-protocol): avoid intermediate String allocations in RespFrame::encode_into

RespFrame integer/length encoding was going through `.to_string()`, which
heap-allocates a fresh `String` on every frame and every bulk-string header
and every array header just to `extend_from_slice` its bytes into the output
buffer. For a simple `*3\r\n$3\r\nSET\r\n$5\r\nhello\r\n$5\r\nworld\r\n`
reply that is three independent `String` allocations per frame, on the hot
wire-encoding path that every response flows through.

Switch to `write!(out, "{n}")` via `std::io::Write`. The `Vec<u8>` Write
impl writes digit bytes directly into the output vector using a stack
buffer inside `itoa`-style formatting, so there is no intermediate
allocation. Same wire output byte-for-byte.

Three encoding sites converted: `Integer`, `BulkString(Some(_))` length
header, and `Array(Some(_))` length header. The `_ = write!(...)` discard
is intentional — writing to a `Vec<u8>` cannot fail, so the `io::Result`
carries no information.

Pure perf refactor. Measured as part of the benchmark gate work, this is
one of the cheap wins on the path between frankenredis and parity with
legacy Redis on the RESP hot loop.
